claim: Mexico friendshoring premium at severe risk of reversal — specific mispricing in manufacturing equity multiples that haven't fully reflected 25% tariff impairment despite 15-20% MXN depreciation
needs: Monitoring of tariff levels on Mexico under evolving political dynamics; USMCA 2026 renegotiation outcome; comparison of manufacturing equity multiples vs fundamentals under sustained tariff regime

claim: Strategic geopolitical zombies — subsidy-dependent firms ($150-250B committed via CHIPS/IRA/defense) suppressing aggregate default statistics while building contingent fiscal liabilities, with semiconductor fabs financed 50-100bp tighter than standalone metrics justify
needs: Base-rate evidence for subsidy-dependent default dynamics; measurement of political durability of specific programs; tracking of disbursement vs commitment rates and scale of contingent fiscal liabilities

claim: Value factor receives a 50-150bp annualized tailwind from deglobalization favoring tangible-asset-heavy firms
needs: Formal decomposition of value premium under deglobalization regime vs other regimes; resolution of whether subsidy dependence creates offsetting fragility; evidence on whether AI-driven intangible premium offsets tangible-asset advantage

claim: Weaponization of interdependence has a 0/2 historical base rate for preserving incumbent system centrality, with transitions taking 20-40 years, implying dollar reserve share decline accelerates
needs: Larger historical sample (n=2 is insufficient); analysis of whether modern financial infrastructure stickiness fundamentally changes transition dynamics; identification of threshold at which USD reserve share decline produces systemic effects

claim: Momentum factor faces 1.5-2.5x crash risk amplification from discrete trade policy shocks, with four documented 4+ sigma episodes since 2018
needs: Larger sample than n=4 for tail-risk estimation; formal statistical test for structural break in momentum crash frequency; analysis of interaction between trade policy shocks and momentum's overlap with AI-concentrated names

claim: Bimodal HY default distribution: 2.5% benign vs 7-9% adverse mode, with market pricing exclusively embedding the left mode at HY OAS ~350-400bp
needs: Formal probability weight estimation for each mode; identification of leading indicators that distinguish which mode is materializing in real-time; sector-level default rate decomposition under each scenario
